customer_anxiety_profile: Informed, active shopper who has seen multiple companies and has a clear purchase preference (no PPA). Primary anxiety is time efficiency — he does not want to bring himself and his wife to an in-person meeting without pre-screening the offer. Secondary anxiety is delivery-fee opacity (the Ticketmaster grievance). He is not hostile; he is protective of his and his wife's time.
unresolved_concerns: Guillermo preferred a phone call before the in-person visit — he accepted the in-person format but was not fully satisfied with the resolution. He may arrive guarded.
Wife's attendance on Tuesday at 5PM is not confirmed. Guillermo flagged that their schedules are difficult to align. Closer should confirm both will be present before the appointment.

Guillermo asked for the design and cost to be emailed before the appointment. Pike said he would 'maybe request it' — closer should be prepared to address this expectation directly at the start of the call.
sensitive_topics_raised: Hard PPA objection — Guillermo stated explicitly he is not interested in PPA and asked for it to be noted. Do not open with or pivot to PPA framing.
Delivery-fee grievance — Guillermo expressed genuine frustration with utility fee opacity (Ticketmaster analogy at 03:25). This is the emotional anchor for the pitch.

Bill data is partial and possibly ambiguous: January reading is 2124 kWh (clearly stated). February–March reading is noted as '12/30' and March–April as '11/12' in slash notation — these may be 1230 kWh and 1112 kWh respectively, but the transcript notation is ambiguous. Verify the full 12-month usage at the appointment before finalizing the design.
exact_promises_rep_made: No out-of-pocket cost on any option ('no matter what you're looking at, that I do know that for sure. There is not a pocket cost or any of these no matter what you look at with us') — closer must verify this holds for the purchase/loan options Guillermo is interested in.
Pike said he would 'put in the notes' that Guillermo wants to know about the Powerwall and the PPA-vs-purchase distinction — closer should address both proactively.
Pike said the closer would collect the remaining monthly usage data at the appointment to complete the full-year design.
Pike said the closer would bring designs to compare against the other companies' proposals Guillermo has already seen.
closer_should_know: Guillermo's stated purchase criterion is full consumption coverage ('having the panels that I need to cover the consumption for the house') — open with that frame, not with a generic design walkthrough. He is an informed shopper who has seen multiple companies, has a hard no on PPA, and will be evaluating Ion's proposal against existing quotes. Confirm the wife is on the call before starting — she is on title and her absence makes this a one-leg close.
